comment_text:
9.2/10. REALLY enjoyed it. and doug being back was the icing. a few small story gripes, but overall i felt a range of emotions and will watch it again in the future. i could really picture myself in this story, as ive had a difficult life as a self made orphan. i wont share too much in detail, but this one resonated with me more than the usual witcher playthrough or watch. also the love theme stung a bit as i just had a romantic split.

i wish it wasnt tied to netflix, and im worried about the future. also a little confused by the mixed reviews on this one? i think netflix < henry caville too, and im not hyped for season 3 live action, but this wasnt that.

comment_text:
Once again Netflix is showing complete lack of respect for the source material. This is dumbed down, shallow husk of the original short story titled "A little bit of sacrifice". The original is loosely based on "Little mermaid". Nuanced and thought provoking but also playful and funny. A bit sad but even in this sadness there is hope.

Netflix's version is completely devoided of original's witts and charm. It seems like target audience was briefed to the writers as old enough to be presented with semi-adult content but at the same time immature and the childlike. What a disgrace.

video_title: The Witcher: Sirens of The Deep | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text: The anime adaptation of the game is an absolute disaster. It was incredibly difficult to sit through, with a plot that felt rushed and poorly written. The characters, so beloved in the games and books, were reduced to hollow versions of themselves, making their arcs unrecognizable and disjointed. Whoever directed this mess should resign immediately; this kind of incompetence does a disservice to the source material. Someone with the talent of Vince Gilligan or Stephen Butchard would have been a much better fit to bring Geralt’s world to life or simply let the director of the games run the show. The Nightmare of the Wolf was a breath of fresh air in comparison, and it’s baffling why they keep mismanaging Geralt’s stories like this. Disappointed! Literally story stolen from The Little Mermaid

 But no, Netflix turned it into Aquaman and a great battle between the sea world and the land world! Great armies clashing for domination! Well, jprdl

comment_text:
This is great, I mean it.

And also, he is too HOT! How are we going to reflect on the moral dilemas as the books intended when our main protagonist is sculpted by Apolo's asscheeks?

Recent adaptations of the witcher, both in videogames and tv, really tried to be a love letter to the franchise. And some succeeded, going above and beyond. Cavill's job has been great to open up even more the discussion about lore fidelity, respecting the sources, and listening to audiences. He managed to become the new face of white wolf, and another good example of ethics in the industry. And yet, we still need aesthetically unwelcoming main characters to invite reflection about their actions. After all, the universe of the witcher is a universe of moral ambiguity, and about the challenges of remaining neutral.
